description: Mexican drug trafficking organization based in Ciudad Juárez, Chihuahua. Founded in 1970 by Pablo Acosta Villarreal, it became dominant in central Mexico controlling cocaine trafficking to the US. After the death of leader Amado Carrillo in 1997, the cartel declined and later reemerged as the Nuevo Cartel de Juárez. It remains active with an estimated 8,000 members.

country_links.evidence_quote: haciendo tratos con los más grandes e importantes carteles colombianos de la droga de ese momento:a partir de 1987 con el Cartel de Medellín ... se asociaron con el Cartel de Cali ... se asocio con el Cartel del Norte del Valle
country_links.context: The Juárez Cartel made deals with Colombian cartels including the Medellín Cartel, Cali Cartel, and Norte del Valle Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: controlando un gran porcentaje del tráfico de cocaína desde México hacia los Estados Unidos.
country_links.context: The Juárez Cartel controls a large percentage of cocaine trafficking from Mexico into the United States.
